text: Introversion (I): Focuses on internal thoughts, feelings, and ideas. Gains energy from solitude. Enjoys deep, one-on-one conversations.
Extraversion (E): Focuses on the external world, people, and activities. Gains energy from social interactions and group settings. Highly outgoing.
Sensing (S): Gathers information through the five senses. Focuses on concrete facts, details, and present realities rather than the abstract.
Intuition (N): Gathers information through patterns, impressions, and possibilities. Focuses on the abstract, future potential, and underlying meanings.
Thinking (T): Makes decisions based on logic, objective analysis, and cause-and-effect reasoning. Values fairness and consistency above emotional alignment.
Feeling (F): Makes decisions based on personal values, empathy, and social harmony. Values compassion and considers the impact on people.
Judging (J): Prefers structure, planning, and organization. Likes to have matters settled, schedules adhered to, and decisions made promptly.
Perceiving (P): Prefers flexibility, spontaneity, and keeping options open. Adapts easily to new information and dislikes rigid constraints.
